product.one_line_description: Vercel REST API allows programmatic interaction with a Vercel account to manage deployments, domains, secrets, and environment variables.

authentication.auth_summary: Authentication to the Vercel REST API requires Vercel Access Tokens, which are included in the Authorization header as a Bearer token.

developer_access.access_notes: Vercel Access Tokens, required for API authentication, can be created and managed by the user in their account settings without human approval.

api_surface.api_summary: Vercel provides a comprehensive REST API over HTTP/1 and HTTP/2 for programmatic interaction with Vercel accounts, offering a broad range of endpoints covering access groups, aliases, deployments, domains, environment variables, projects, teams, and much more.

mcp.mcp_summary: No information about Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers or related functionalities was found in the provided source.

buildability.verdict_reasoning: Self-serve developer access is verified with a broad/moderate documented public API.

evidence_pool.claim: Interact programmatically with your Vercel account using the SDK or direct HTTP requests. You can deploy new versions of web applications, manage custom domains, retrieve information about deployments, and manage secrets and environment variables for projects.
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: Interact programmatically with your Vercel account using the SDK or direct HTTP requests. You can deploy new versions of web applications, manage custom domains, retrieve information about deployments, and manage secrets and environment variables for projects.

Vercel Access Tokens are required to authenticate and use the Vercel API. Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

`Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>`
Create and manage Access Tokens in your [account settings](https://vercel.com/account/tokens).
Create and manage Access Tokens in your [account settings](https://vercel.com/account/tokens).

evidence_pool.claim: Create and manage Access Tokens in your [account settings](https://vercel.com/account/tokens).
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: Create and manage Access Tokens in your [account settings](https://vercel.com/account/tokens).

Vercel REST API

# [Using the REST API](#using-the-rest-api)[](#using-the-rest-api)

Interact programmatically with your Vercel account using the SDK or direct HTTP requests.
... The API is exposed as an HTTP/1 and HTTP/2 service over SSL. All endpoints live under the URL `https://api.vercel.com` and follow the REST architecture.

Browse all available REST API endpoints grouped by category.

product.one_line_description: Vercel REST API allows programmatic interaction with a Vercel account to manage deployments, domains, secrets, and environment variables.

authentication.auth_summary: Authentication to the Vercel REST API requires Vercel Access Tokens, which are included in the Authorization header as a Bearer token.

developer_access.access_notes: Vercel Access Tokens, required for API authentication, can be created and managed by the user in their account settings without human approval.

api_surface.api_summary: Vercel provides a comprehensive REST API over HTTP/1 and HTTP/2 for programmatic interaction with Vercel accounts, offering a broad range of endpoints covering access groups, aliases, deployments, domains, environment variables, projects, teams, and much more.

mcp.mcp_summary: No information about Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers or related functionalities was found in the provided source.

buildability.verdict_reasoning: Self-serve developer access is verified with a broad/moderate documented public API.
